description: Rooms, furniture, household items, and home life

items.notes: A building/house. Partitive: taloa. 'Talo' refers to the physical building, while 'koti' means the concept of home.
examples: Talo on iso. — The house is big.
Meillä on punainen talo. — We have a red house.

items.notes: Home (as a concept, where you live). Inessive: kotona (at home). Illative: kotiin (to home). Elative: kotoa (from home).
examples: Olen kotona. — I am at home.
Menen kotiin. — I'm going home.

items.notes: An apartment or flat. Partitive: asuntoa. Often used in compound 'kerrostaloasunto' (apartment in a block of flats).
examples: Asunto on toisessa kerroksessa. — The apartment is on the second floor.
Haen uutta asuntoa. — I'm looking for a new apartment.

items.notes: A room. Partitive: huonetta. Very common in compounds: makuuhuone, olohuone, kylpyhuone.
examples: Asunnossa on kolme huonetta. — The apartment has three rooms.
Huone on valoisa. — The room is bright.

items.notes: Kitchen. Note the 'ö' at the end. Derived from 'keittää' (to cook). Partitive: keittiötä.
examples: Keittiö on pieni. — The kitchen is small.
Syömme keittiössä. — We eat in the kitchen.

items.notes: Compound word: makuu (lying down, sleeping) + huone (room). Partitive: makuuhuonetta.
examples: Makuuhuoneessa on iso sänky. — There is a big bed in the bedroom.
Talossa on kaksi makuuhuonetta. — The house has two bedrooms.

items.notes: Compound word: olo (being, staying) + huone (room). The main room for relaxing and socializing.
examples: Katsomme televisiota olohuoneessa. — We watch television in the living room.
Olohuone on kodikas. — The living room is cozy.

items.notes: Compound word: kylpy (bath) + huone (room). In Finland, the bathroom typically includes a shower but not always a bathtub.
examples: Kylpyhuone on siisti. — The bathroom is clean.
Kylpyhuoneessa on suihku. — There is a shower in the bathroom.

items.notes: Door. Partitive: ovea. Short and common word. 'Avaa ovi' = open the door, 'sulje ovi' = close the door.
examples: Avaa ovi! — Open the door!
Ovi on lukossa. — The door is locked.

items.notes: Window. Partitive: ikkunaa. Finnish windows are typically triple-glazed due to the cold climate.
examples: Avaa ikkuna! — Open the window!
Ikkunasta näkee järven. — You can see the lake from the window.

items.notes: Floor (the surface you walk on inside a building). Partitive: lattiaa. Not to be confused with 'kerros' (floor/story of a building).
examples: Lattia on puhdas. — The floor is clean.
Kissa makaa lattialla. — The cat is lying on the floor.

items.notes: Ceiling (inside) or roof (outside). Partitive: kattoa. Context determines meaning. 'Katon alla' = under the roof.
examples: Katto on valkoinen. — The ceiling is white.
Lintu istuu katolla. — A bird is sitting on the roof.

items.notes: Wall. Note the 'ä'. Partitive: seinää. 'Seinällä' = on the wall (adessive case).
examples: Seinä on sininen. — The wall is blue.
Taulu on seinällä. — The painting is on the wall.

items.notes: Stairs/staircase. Always plural in Finnish. Singular 'porras' means a single step. Genitive plural: portaiden.
examples: Portaat ovat jyrkät. — The stairs are steep.
Menen portaita ylös. — I go up the stairs.

items.notes: Table. Note both 'ö' and 'ä'. Partitive: pöytää. 'Ruokapöytä' = dining table, 'kirjoituspöytä' = desk.
examples: Pöytä on keittiössä. — The table is in the kitchen.
Laita lautanen pöydälle. — Put the plate on the table.

items.notes: Chair. Partitive: tuolia. 'Nojatuoli' = armchair (literally: leaning chair).
examples: Istu tuolille! — Sit on the chair!
Keittiössä on neljä tuolia. — There are four chairs in the kitchen.

items.notes: Bed. Note the 'ä'. Partitive: sänkyä. 'Parisänky' = double bed, 'kerrossänky' = bunk bed.
examples: Sänky on pehmeä. — The bed is soft.
Menen sänkyyn. — I go to bed.

items.notes: Sofa or couch. Partitive: sohvaa. 'Sohvalla' = on the sofa. A loanword from Swedish.
examples: Istun sohvalla. — I'm sitting on the sofa.
Sohva on olohuoneessa. — The sofa is in the living room.

items.notes: Cabinet, cupboard, or closet. Partitive: kaappia. Common in compounds: 'vaatekaappi' (wardrobe), 'jääkaappi' (fridge).
examples: Laita vaatteet kaappiin. — Put the clothes in the closet.
Kaappi on täynnä. — The cabinet is full.

items.notes: Lamp or light fixture. Partitive: lamppua. 'Pöytälamppu' = table lamp, 'kattolamppu' = ceiling lamp.
examples: Laita lamppu päälle. — Turn on the lamp.
Lamppu on rikki. — The lamp is broken.

items.notes: Mirror. Partitive: peiliä. 'Katsoa peiliin' = to look in the mirror.
examples: Peili on kylpyhuoneessa. — The mirror is in the bathroom.
Katson peiliin. — I look in the mirror.

items.notes: Rug or carpet. Partitive: mattoa. In Finland, 'mattojen pesu' (rug washing) at lakeside docks is a summer tradition.
examples: Matto on lattialla. — The rug is on the floor.
Punainen matto on olohuoneessa. — The red carpet is in the living room.

items.notes: Compound word: jää (ice) + kaappi (cabinet). Literally 'ice cabinet'. Partitive: jääkaappia.
examples: Maito on jääkaapissa. — The milk is in the refrigerator.
Jääkaappi on tyhjä. — The refrigerator is empty.

items.notes: Stove or cooktop. Partitive: liettä (consonant gradation: s→t). Can also mean a hearth historically.
examples: Kattila on liedellä. — The pot is on the stove.
Liesi on kuuma. — The stove is hot.

items.notes: Oven. Partitive: uunia. Also used for fireplace ovens common in Finnish homes. 'Mikrouuni' = microwave.
examples: Laita kakku uuniin. — Put the cake in the oven.
Uuni on kuuma. — The oven is hot.

items.notes: Compound word: pesu (washing) + kone (machine). Partitive: pesukonetta. 'Astianpesukone' = dishwasher.
examples: Pesukone on kylpyhuoneessa. — The washing machine is in the bathroom.
Laita pyykit pesukoneeseen. — Put the laundry in the washing machine.

items.notes: Television. Often abbreviated as 'TV' or colloquially 'telkkari'. Partitive: televisiota.
examples: Televisio on olohuoneessa. — The television is in the living room.
Katsomme televisiota illalla. — We watch television in the evening.

items.notes: Compound word: tieto (knowledge, information) + kone (machine). Literally 'knowledge machine'. Partitive: tietokonetta. 'Kannettava tietokone' = laptop.
examples: Tietokone on pöydällä. — The computer is on the table.
Tarvitsen uuden tietokoneen. — I need a new computer.

items.notes: Phone. Consonant stem: puhelime- (e.g., genitive: puhelimen). Derived from 'puhua' (to speak). 'Matkapuhelin' = mobile phone.
examples: Puhelin soi. — The phone is ringing.
Missä on puhelimeni? — Where is my phone?

items.notes: Key. Consonant stem: avaime- (e.g., genitive: avaimen, plural: avaimet). Related to 'avata' (to open).
examples: Missä avaimet ovat? — Where are the keys?
Unohdin avaimen kotiin. — I forgot the key at home.

items.notes: Lock. Partitive: lukkoa. 'Lukossa' = locked (inessive). Related verb: 'lukita' (to lock).
examples: Ovi on lukossa. — The door is locked.
Lukko on rikki. — The lock is broken.

items.notes: Compound word: puu (tree) + tarha (yard, enclosure). Partitive: puutarhaa. 'Puutarhuri' = gardener.
examples: Puutarhassa kasvaa kukkia. — Flowers grow in the garden.
Puutarha on kaunis kesällä. — The garden is beautiful in summer.

items.notes: Balcony. Partitive: parveketta. Very common in Finnish apartment buildings. 'Lasitettu parveke' = glazed balcony (common in Finland).
examples: Istun parvekkeella. — I'm sitting on the balcony.
Parvekkeelta näkee puiston. — You can see the park from the balcony.

items.notes: Elevator/lift. Partitive: hissiä. A loanword from Swedish 'hiss'.
examples: Menen hissillä. — I take the elevator.
Hissi on rikki. — The elevator is broken.

items.notes: Floor or story of a building (not the surface). Partitive: kerrosta. 'Ensimmäinen kerros' = first floor. Also means 'layer'. Not to be confused with 'lattia' (floor surface).
examples: Asun kolmannessa kerroksessa. — I live on the third floor.
Talossa on viisi kerrosta. — The building has five floors.
